_comment: Absolute performance budgets — the single source of truth for all three perf scopes (DB-layer runner, k6 HTTP, BenchmarkDotNet). A scope exits non-zero when a measured value breaches its budget. Most budgets are calibrated from a 1M-row run on a dev machine (2026-06); RECALIBRATE on your target/CI hardware (set each ~20-30% above the observed p95/mean). A missing key = measure but never fail.
_comment_stats: The statistics aggregates (statsSummary/statsLatencyPercentiles/statsTokenUsage/statsModelBreakdown/statsCostEstimate/statsCallTrends) translate to server-side GROUP BY/sum/percentile_cont — they do NOT client-evaluate. Their cost is one scan of the windowed rows: sub-second at 1M after planner statistics are current. The ~3.7-4.4s seen on the first 1M run (issue #246) was a stale-statistics artifact — a freshly bulk-seeded table with no ANALYZE made the planner pick a nested-loop plan that random-read the whole table; the same SQL runs ~270-880ms once analyzed (3452ms -> 478ms on EXPLAIN). The seeder now ANALYZEs after the bulk load and migration TuneAgentCallAutovacuum keeps stats fresh in production, so these budgets are real measured p95 + ~30-45% headroom on a 1M dev run (2026-06). The three heaviest (latencyPercentiles ~880ms full sort, tokenUsage/callTrends ~780-880ms bucketed full scans) are scan-bound — no index helps a full-window aggregate; sub-second at 10M+ would need pre-aggregated rollups. RECALIBRATE on your CI hardware (~20-30% above observed p95).
_comment_testRunStats: testRunStatsBySuite/testRunStatsBySuitePage guard the suite-scoped TestRunStats projection query (issue #253). The test-suites controller previously materialized the whole TestRunStatsEntity table and filtered in memory on every suites list + single-suite GET; the fix pushes the suite set into SQL (WHERE SuiteId IN (...)), backed by the existing index on TestRunStatsEntity.SuiteId. The query returns only the rows for the requested suite(s), so it is an index range read independent of total table size. These two budgets are UNCALIBRATED PLACEHOLDERS (set conservatively for an index-scoped read) — they were added with the fix but the harness has not been run at scale; RECALIBRATE on a full ~1M / 25k-test-run seed (set ~20-30% above the observed p95).
